findings.description: Header block formatting is simplified and alignment differs from the PDF.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF places governor/secretary information on the left and director/phone on the right around the centered date, while the HTML stacks these items vertically on the left under the address line.

findings.description: Inline label formatting is not fully preserved in the salutation block.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF shows 'Attention:' followed by two bold indented lines and 'Subject:' with a more tabular alignment; the HTML keeps the text but compresses spacing/alignment and presents the labels more inline.

findings.description: Signature image appearance differs noticeably from the PDF.
findings.evidence: On page 4, the PDF shows a handwritten signature on a light background; the HTML renders it as a darker black rectangular image, though it remains in the correct section above the typed name.
